channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senior professionals face reduced salaries as companies favour junior AI-literate staff
item.description: Deirdre Hosking, a former senior HR executive in the finance sector, took a career break last year, expecting the job market for leadership positions to rebound. Upon her return, she discovered that t...

item.title: Queensland coach Billy Slater outlines Maroons team for Game One, opting for Kalyn Ponga at fullback
item.description: Queensland coach Billy Slater has revealed his Maroons squad for Game One of the State of Origin series, making some surprising selections. Notably, Slater has chosen Kalyn Ponga to start at fullback,...

item.title: Thunder and Spurs Set for Highly Anticipated Western Conference Finals
item.description: The Western Conference finals are set to feature an exciting showdown between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the San Antonio Spurs. The Thunder, led by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, finished the regular sea...

item.title: Sultan of Sokoto Announces Start of Zulhijja on Monday, May 18, 2026
item.description: The Sultan of Sokoto, Muhammad Sa’ad Abubakar, wey also dey serve as President-General of the Nigeria Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs, don officially declare say Monday, May 18, 2026, go mark the ...

item.title: Fabio Di Giannantonio takes victory in disrupted Catalan MotoGP round after multiple crashes and red flags
item.description: In a chaotic MotoGP race at the Catalan Grand Prix, VR46 Racing’s Fabio Di Giannantonio secured his second career victory under extraordinary circumstances. The event was red-flagged twice due to seri...

item.title: Trust and Accountability at the Elon Musk vs. OpenAI Trial
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI has reached its closing arguments, centering on whether OpenAI acted improperly as it shifted toward a more profit-oriented model. A major theme highlighted duri...

item.title: FIFA and Iranian Football Federation Confirm Iran's Participation in 2026 World Cup
item.description: FIFA secretary general Mattias Grafstrom described the recent meeting with the Iranian Football Federation in Istanbul as excellent and constructive, aimed at confirming Iran’s participation in the 20...

item.title: Saranya Subramanian’s Debut Poetry Collection Explores Urban Memories and Human Absence
item.description: Saranya Subramanian’s debut poetry collection, 'Absent People, Absent Places', presents a reflective and inventive engagement with the cityscape and its inhabitants. Her poems capture the subtle inter...

item.title: VAR technical issue delays first leg of Sundowns vs AS FAR Champions League final
item.description: On Sunday, the 2025-26 Caf Champions League first leg final between Mamelodi Sundowns and AS FAR at Loftus Versfeld faced an unexpected disruption due to a VAR (Video Assistant Referee) malfunction. A...

item.title: Canadian Passenger on Hantavirus-Affected Cruise Ship Tests Positive, Officials Report
item.description: A Canadian who was on the MV Hondius, a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak in April, has tested presumptively positive for the virus. The individual, from Yukon, is one of four Canadians cu...

item.title: Jonathan Gjoshe reflects on surviving train knife attack and rebuilding football career
item.description: Jonathan Gjoshe, a 23-year-old footballer for Scunthorpe United, has spoken publicly for the first time about surviving a brutal knife attack on a train in Cambridgeshire on 1 November last year. Trav...

item.title: Outdoor Learning in Pune Schools Enhances Students’ Understanding of the Environment
item.description: In Pune, there is a growing interest in educational approaches that connect children more closely with nature. Traditional classroom-based learning often limits students’ engagement with environmental...

item.title: Nanoparticle Therapy Restores Brain Waste Clearance and Reverses Alzheimer’s Symptoms in Mice
item.description: Researchers from the Institute for Bioengineering of Catalonia and West China Hospital Sichuan University, along with collaborators in the UK, have developed a nanoparticle-based therapy that reversed...

item.title: Aaron Rai becomes first Englishman in over a century to win US PGA Championship
item.description: Aaron Rai, a 31-year-old golfer from Wolverhampton, achieved a historic victory at the US PGA Championship, marking the first English win in over a century. Rai, who previously had a best major finish...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of 'Badlands' (1973) Featuring Martin Sheen and Sissy Spacek
item.description: The article provides an in-depth look at the 1973 American neo-noir crime drama 'Badlands,' which marked Terrence Malick's directorial debut. Starring Martin Sheen as Kit and Sissy Spacek as Holly, th...

item.title: Motorcyclist in Barrie Caught Driving Nearly Triple the Speed Limit on Main Road
item.description: A motorcyclist in Barrie, Ontario, was recently recorded travelling at an alarming speed of 169 km/h in a 60 km/h zone on a major local road. The incident has drawn significant attention from both loc...

item.title: Sinner Claims Sixth Consecutive Masters 1000 Title at Italian Open
item.description: Naija tennis fans dey reason as Jannik Sinner don show say e get serious skill for court. The world number one don win the Italian Open after e beat Casper Ruud 6-4, 6-4 for the final, making am sixth...

item.title: Argentine Organisation Rehomes Laboratory Rats and Mice Through Annual Event
item.description: In Buenos Aires, Argentina, an annual event called Ratapalooza brings attention to the adoption of laboratory rats and mice that are no longer needed for scientific research. Organised by Team Ratas, ...

item.title: Dennis Hopper: Actor, Director, and Photographer of the 1960s
item.description: Dennis Hopper (1936–2010) was a defining figure of the 1960s, known not only for his acting but also for his work behind the camera as a director and photographer. After early career setbacks and a br...

item.title: USM Alger Wins CAF Confederation Cup After Penalty Shootout Against Zamalek SC
item.description: USM Alger claimed their second TotalEnergies CAF Confederation Cup title after an intense final against Zamalek SC that ended in an 8-7 penalty shootout victory. The two-legged final concluded with a ...
